enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Aandipatti, a melodious track from the movie Dharmadurai, captures the essence of heartfelt emotions and scenic beauty. Composed by Yuvan Shankar Raja and sung by Senthil Dass and Surmukhi, this song is a perfect blend of soothing tunes and meaningful lyrics. Experience the magic of Tamil cinema's musical brilliance with this enchanting number.
stanzaMeanings: Dharmadurai Aandipatti Song Lyrics Singer(s): Senthil Dass & Surmukhi Music:Yuvan.
Aandipatti dreams are carried away by the wind. The girl with the bow captivates me.
Oh precious golden girl, why don't you trust me completely? I swear on my head, yet my fear doesn't fade.
Your youth and your saree are adorned by a fair maiden.
Why was this poor smile chosen for this crisis?
A fair maiden touches money and runs away in disgust.
She lives by selling cucumbers and guarding her home.
We have tied the sacred thread and made a promise.
Step back and don't cross the line, it's a promise.
If touched slightly, the pot makes a sound.
The fox that brushes against the ground doesn't stop.
You are like the yellow Karuvelam flower, without any dust or dirt. I will tie the yellow sacred thread on you, my dear child.
Will the heat become real for me? Will it become real?
Does the star doubt the water below it?
The poor girl is deceived and leaves.
Is it enough if I write and give it to you?
I called all the elephants in the village, addressing them as 'Anna, Anna'.
Now I call only you as 'Maama'.
